items.title: Show HN: I built with Claude Code a bookmarks manager with a social cut
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer built and shipped Linkraider, a social bookmarking platform, using Claude Code. It features a fully functional guest mode so users can test it without signing up. Because you are building AI-ingest—which is also a link-filtering and curation tool—this is a direct example of someone shipping a similar concept using your exact primary tool. This week, open Claude Code and ask it to help you implement a 'guest demo' state for AI-ingest so people can try your tool instantly.

items.title: Show HN: Let agents send/receive SMS using your old Android phone
items.why_it_matters: A developer vibe coded a simple Android app and MCP relay server that lets AI agents send and receive SMS using an old Android phone. It is a highly practical, low-cost alternative to setting up complex Twilio APIs for sending notifications or receiving OTP codes. This week, download the open-source relay server and use Claude Code to write a script that sends a text notification to your phone whenever you make a new Git commit.

items.title: Show HN: Git worktrees and evidence gates for Codex and Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This tool automates Git worktrees and evidence gates specifically for developers using Codex and Claude Code. Because you are currently learning Git commit discipline on your AI-ingest repository, this helps keep your agent-driven code changes isolated and organized without manual Git overhead. This week, clone this repository and run it locally to manage your feature branches while vibe coding.

items.title: Show HN: Open-source Antigravity plugin for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This open-source plugin adds extra slash commands directly inside your Claude Code terminal, including model flags, deep research, and code reviews. It makes your daily editor more powerful by letting you switch models or trigger deep reviews without leaving your CLI. This week, install the plugin and run the new code review command on your AI-ingest codebase to find optimization opportunities.

items.title: Show HN: CWC scans your Claude Code history and auto-builds agent workflows
items.why_it_matters: CWC scans your local Claude Code history and automatically generates reusable agent workflows based on your actual coding patterns. It helps you transition from manually typing the same prompts to automating repetitive setup tasks. This week, run CWC on your AI-ingest directory to analyze your prompt history and generate a single-command workflow for setting up new Next.js pages.

items.title: Show HN: One-click fork of 'Everything Claude Code' onto an isolated microVM
items.why_it_matters: This template lets you run Claude Code inside an isolated microVM with one click. It keeps your local machine safe from unintended agent actions while giving the model a secure, sandboxed environment to run and test your code. This week, spin up this microVM template to test a experimental feature for AI-ingest without risking your local development environment.
